join_order.note: Trivial: direct array since s_suppkey is dense integer 1..100K
Use partsupp_ps_partkey index to skip 8M-440K=7.56M partsupp rows

join_order.build: orders(15M rows) -> o_orderkey to year compact hash map
join_order.note: Build before lineitem scan; year extracted via O(1) date lookup table

join_order.probe: lineitem(60M rows) — single parallel fused scan
join_order.join_key: l_partkey(bitset) AND l_suppkey(flat_array) AND (l_partkey,l_suppkey)(composite_map) AND l_orderkey(year_map)
join_order.output: thread_local flat_2d_array[25][10] of int64_t profit sums
join_order.note: Part bitset eliminates ~94.5% of lineitem rows at first check; only ~3.3M rows advance to subsequent lookups

filter_pushdown.action: build bitset(2000001) of qualifying p_partkey values
use partsupp_ps_partkey hash index to probe only matching rows
first check in probe loop; eliminates 94.5% of rows immediately

logical_plan.amount_computation: int64_t scaled arithmetic: amount_scaled = l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) - ps_supplycost * l_quantity; divide by 10000 for final decimal output
logical_plan.sort_strategy: std::sort on 175 result rows (nation ASC, o_year DESC) — trivial due to tiny output

data_structures.nation_lookup: flat_array<NationEntry>[26], indexed by n_nationkey; NationEntry = {nation_idx: uint8_t, n_name: string_view}; fits in L1 cache
data_structures.supplier_nation_array: flat_array<uint8_t>[100001], indexed by s_suppkey (dense 1..100K), value = nation_idx (0..24); 100KB total, fits in L2 cache
data_structures.nation_names_array: flat_array<std::string>[25], indexed by nation_idx; loaded once from nation scan
data_structures.part_filter_bitset: bitset of size 2000001 (250KB, fits in L3 cache); set bit for each qualifying p_partkey; O(1) test per lineitem row
data_structures.partsupp_composite_map: compact open-addressing hash map, key=int64_t packed as ps_partkey*100001LL+ps_suppkey, value=int64_t ps_supplycost; pre-sized for ~440K entries at 0.5 load factor (880K slots); multiply-shift hash function
data_structures.orders_year_map: compact open-addressing hash map, key=int32_t o_orderkey, value=int16_t year; pre-sized for 15M entries at 0.5 load factor (32M slots, ~384MB); multiply-shift hash; parallel partitioned build
data_structures.thread_local_aggregation: flat_2d_array<int64_t>[25][10] per thread; 25 nation_idx slots × 10 year slots (year_offset = year - 1992); 2KB per thread, 128KB total across 64 threads; NO synchronization during probe

build_phases.phase_A_nation: sequential scan of 25 rows (trivial); build nation flat array
build_phases.phase_B_supplier: sequential scan of 100K rows; join with nation array; build suppkey->nation_idx flat array; ~1ms
build_phases.phase_C_part: parallel scan of 2M part rows (64 threads), p_name substring search for 'green'; qualifying p_partkey values atomically set in shared bitset (lock-free: bitset word CAS or thread-local bitsets merged); ~10ms
build_phases.phase_D_partsupp: iterate over ~110K qualifying p_partkey values from bitset; for each, use partsupp_ps_partkey hash_multi_value index to retrieve ps_suppkey and ps_supplycost column values at indexed positions; build composite hash map; ~15ms
build_phases.phase_E_orders: parallel scan of 15M orders rows (64 threads); extract year via YEAR_TABLE[o_orderdate] O(1) lookup; insert into orders_year_map using partitioned concurrent build (16 partitions by o_orderkey % 16, each partition owned by subset of threads); ~30ms

parallelism.build_parallelism: phases A-D sequential or lightly parallel; phase E parallel partitioned build
parallelism.probe_parallelism: 64-thread parallel scan over lineitem; each thread processes ~937K rows; thread-local flat_2d_array aggregation with no contention
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — probe phase has zero synchronization; final merge of 64 arrays is sequential (175 additions per thread = 11.2K total additions, <1ms)
parallelism.note: All hash table BUILD completes before parallel PROBE begins; no race conditions on read-only hash tables during probe

index_usage.purpose: For each of ~110K qualifying p_partkey values, directly retrieve matching partsupp row positions; avoids scanning 8M partsupp rows; reduces I/O from ~200MB to ~11MB

physical_plan.lineitem_scan_order: check l_partkey bitset FIRST (eliminates 94.5% of rows) before loading other columns in the same cache line; load all 6 columns together since they are all needed for qualifying rows
physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for lineitem and orders scans; random_access_with_prefetch for orders_year_map probe (hash table lookups); sequential for partsupp index reads
physical_plan.date_extraction: O(1) YEAR_TABLE[epoch_day] lookup table (gendb::extract_year from date_utils.h); precomputed once at startup; eliminates loop-based extraction overhead on 15M orders rows

key_optimizations: part bitset eliminates 94.5% of lineitem rows at O(1) cost per row
partsupp_ps_partkey index avoids scanning 8M rows (reads only ~440K via index)
flat_array suppkey->nation_idx avoids hash join for 100K-row supplier table
flat_2d_array[25][10] aggregation — 175 groups fit in L1 cache, zero hash overhead
O(1) date extraction via YEAR_TABLE prevents 30-iteration loop on 15M rows
64-thread parallel lineitem scan with zero-contention thread-local aggregation
all build structures read-only during probe phase — no synchronization overhead
